summary: OnPaintSurface is not called on iOS when InvalidateSurface() is invoked from a background thread (System.Timers.Timer), because iOS UIKit requires SetNeedsDisplay() to be called on the main thread, while Android's Invalidate() is thread-safe.

bugSignals.errorMessage: OnPaintSurface not called on iOS after InvalidateSurface()

stepsToReproduce: Create a MAUI or Xamarin.Forms ContentView with an SKCanvasView inside
Start a System.Timers.Timer that calls skiaView.InvalidateSurface() every 1 second
Run on iOS device or simulator
Observe that OnPaintSurface is never called on iOS (works on Android)
reproEvidence.environmentDetails: Visual Studio for Mac 17.5.5, Xamarin.Forms 5.0.0.2578, .NET 6 and 7

repoLinks.description: Related: InvalidateSurface does not trigger OnPaintSurface on iOS device connected to Windows

versionAnalysis.relevanceReason: The iOS handler still calls SetNeedsDisplay() without dispatching to the main thread, meaning this issue persists in current code.

analysis.summary: The reporter calls InvalidateSurface() from a System.Timers.Timer callback, which runs on a ThreadPool thread. On iOS, this results in SetNeedsDisplay() being called off the main thread — a violation of UIKit's threading rules that silently fails. On Android, View.Invalidate() is internally thread-safe. SkiaSharp's iOS handler does not marshal the call to the main thread, causing the silent failure.
analysis.rationale: This is a platform behavioral inconsistency: the same InvalidateSurface() call works on Android but silently fails on iOS when called from a background thread. The iOS UIKit requirement for main-thread UI calls is well-established, but SkiaSharp could defensively dispatch to the main thread in OnInvalidateSurface. The issue is real and reproducible, making it a medium-severity bug.

keySignals.text: The method OnPaintSurface doesn't get call on iOS. I've implemented a loop to cal the OnPaintSurface every 1 second
keySignals.interpretation: The timer fires on a ThreadPool thread; iOS UIKit calls must be on main thread.

keySignals.text: it doesn't work on iOS only on android with both Xamarin Forms and Maui
keySignals.interpretation: Platform inconsistency: Android Invalidate() is thread-safe, iOS SetNeedsDisplay() is not.

keySignals.text: var timer = new System.Timers.Timer(1000); timer.Elapsed += new ElapsedEventHandler(RedrawSquare);
keySignals.interpretation: System.Timers.Timer.Elapsed always fires on a ThreadPool thread, not the main UI thread.

codeInvestigation.finding: OnInvalidateSurface calls handler.PlatformView.SetNeedsDisplay() directly without marshaling to main thread. iOS UIKit requires this to be called from the main thread; calling it from a background thread silently fails.
OnInvalidateSurface calls handler.PlatformView.Invalidate() which Android handles thread-safely internally, explaining why it works on Android but not iOS.
InvalidateSurface() calls Handler?.Invoke(nameof(ISKCanvasView.InvalidateSurface)) — dispatches to the platform handler without any thread check.

workarounds: Use MainThread.BeginInvokeOnMainThread(() => skiaView.InvalidateSurface()) instead of calling InvalidateSurface() directly from the timer callback
Replace System.Timers.Timer with a MAUI Dispatcher timer: Dispatcher.DispatchDelayed / Application.Current.Dispatcher.StartTimer
nextQuestions: Should SkiaSharp defensively dispatch SetNeedsDisplay() to the main thread in the iOS handler?
Does the same threading issue affect SKGLView on iOS?
Does the Xamarin.Forms iOS handler have the same issue?

resolution.hypothesis: The iOS MAUI handler calls SetNeedsDisplay() on a background thread, which silently fails. Adding a main thread dispatch in OnInvalidateSurface for the Apple platform would fix this.

proposals.title: Add main-thread dispatch in iOS handler
proposals.description: Wrap SetNeedsDisplay() call in MainThread.BeginInvokeOnMainThread in SKCanvasViewHandler.Apple.cs to ensure it runs on the UI thread regardless of the calling thread.

proposals.title: Document thread requirement and provide workaround
proposals.description: Add documentation noting that InvalidateSurface() must be called on the main thread on iOS, and suggest using MainThread.BeginInvokeOnMainThread as a workaround.

resolution.recommendedProposal: Add main-thread dispatch in iOS handler
resolution.recommendedReason: Defensive main-thread marshaling makes SkiaSharp consistent across platforms and prevents silent failures for users who reasonably expect InvalidateSurface() to work from background threads as it does on Android.

actionability.reason: Real platform inconsistency in the iOS handler — InvalidateSurface called from background thread silently fails on iOS but works on Android. Needs investigation to determine if SkiaSharp should add main-thread dispatch defensively.

actions.description: Apply bug, MAUI views, and iOS labels
Explain threading issue and provide workaround
